input.command: kubectl get secret elastic-password -n elasticsearch -o jsonpath='{.data.password}' | base64 -d && echo
kubectl get secret elastic-password-next -n elasticsearch -o jsonpath='{.data.password}' | base64 -d && echo
kubectl get deployment auth-checker -n elasticsearch -o yaml 2>&1

content.content:
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  annotations:
    deployment.kubernetes.io/revision: "1"
    kubectl.kubernetes.io/last-applied-configuration: |
      {"apiVersion":"apps/v1","kind":"Deployment","metadata":{"annotations":{},"name":"auth-checker","namespace":"elasticsearch"},"spec":{"replicas":1,"selector":{"matchLabels":{"app":"auth-checker"}},"template":{"metadata":{"labels":{"app":"auth-checker"}},"spec":{"containers":[{"command":["/bin/sh","-c","sleep infinity\n"],"env":[{"name":"ELASTIC_PASSWORD","valueFrom":{"secretKeyRef":{"key":"password","name":"elastic-password"}}}],"image":"curlimages/curl:8.5.0","name":"checker","readinessProbe":{"exec":{"command":["/bin/sh","-c","curl -s --max-time 5 -u elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD} \\\n  http://es-http.elasticsearch.svc:9200/_security/_authenticate \\\n  | grep -q '\"username\":\"elastic\"'\n"]},"failureThreshold":6,"initialDelaySeconds":10,"periodSeconds":5,"timeoutSeconds":2}}]}}}}
  creationTimestamp: "2026-06-23T23:16:48Z"
  generation: 1
  name: auth-checker
  namespace: elasticsearch
  resourceVersion: "965238"
  uid: f52f826a-adf4-438f-b9b5-35eb97893209
spec:
  progressDeadlineSeconds: 600
  replicas: 1
  revisionHistoryLimit: 10
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: auth-checker
  strategy:
    rollingUpdate:
      maxSurge: 25%
      maxUnavailable: 25%
    type: RollingUpdate
  template:
    metadata:
      creationTimestamp: null
      labels:
        app: auth-checker
    spec:
      containers:
      - command:
        - /bin/sh
        - -c
        - |
          sleep infinity
        env:
        - name: ELASTIC_PASSWORD
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              key: password
              name: elastic-password
        image: curlimages/curl:8.5.0
        imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
        name: checker
        readinessProbe:
          exec:
            command:
            - /bin/sh
            - -c
            - |
              curl -s --max-time 5 -u elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD} \
                http://es-http.elasticsearch.svc:9200/_security/_authenticate \
                | grep -q '"username":"elastic"'
          failureThreshold: 6
          initialDelaySeconds: 10
          periodSeconds: 5
          successThreshold: 1
          timeoutSeconds: 2
        resources: {}
        terminationMessagePath: /dev/termination-log
        terminationMessagePolicy: File
      dnsPolicy: ClusterFirst
      restartPolicy: Always
      schedulerName: default-scheduler
      securityContext: {}
      terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 30

content.thinking:
Let me understand the setup:
1. Current password: `elasticpass`
2. New password: `elasticpass-elastic-password-next`
3. The auth-checker deployment reads the password from the `elastic-password` secret
4. The readiness probe authenticates to ES using this password

I need to:
1. Change the elastic user's password in Elasticsearch itself to the new password
2. Update the `elastic-password` secret to point to the new password
3. Restart the auth-checker so it picks up the new secret

Let me first check how the password is stored in Elasticsearch. Looking at the `es-file-realm-aggregate` and `user-provided-file-realm` secrets since there's a `file-realm-gen` pod.

content.text: Now let me check how Elasticsearch authentication is configured — especially the file realm secrets.

input.command: kubectl get secret es-file-realm-aggregate -n elasticsearch -o yaml 2>&1
kubectl get secret user-provided-file-realm -n elasticsearch -o yaml 2>&1

content.thinking: Let me check how the Elasticsearch cluster is configured, especially how the `elastic` user password is set. The `es-cluster` pods use some form of authentication. Let me check the StatefulSet configuration.

input.command: kubectl get statefulset es-cluster -n elasticsearch -o yaml 2>&1 | head -150

content.content:
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  annotations:
    kubectl.kubernetes.io/last-applied-configuration: |
      {"apiVersion":"apps/v1","kind":"StatefulSet","metadata":{"annotations":{},"name":"es-cluster","namespace":"elasticsearch"},"spec":{"podManagementPolicy":"Parallel","replicas":3,"selector":{"matchLabels":{"app":"es-cluster"}},"serviceName":"es-cluster","template":{"metadata":{"labels":{"app":"es-cluster"}},"spec":{"containers":[{"env":[{"name":"POD_NAME","valueFrom":{"fieldRef":{"fieldPath":"metadata.name"}}},{"name":"ES_JAVA_OPTS","value":"-Xms512m -Xmx512m"},{"name":"ELASTIC_PASSWORD","valueFrom":{"secretKeyRef":{"key":"password","name":"elastic-password"}}}],"image":"docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:8.11.1","name":"elasticsearch","ports":[{"containerPort":9200,"name":"http"},{"containerPort":9300,"name":"transport"}],"readinessProbe":{"exec":{"command":["sh","-c","curl -sk -u \"elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD}\" https://localhost:9200/_cluster/health?wait_for_status=yellow\u0026timeout=1s | grep -q '\"status\":\"'\n"]},"failureThreshold":6,"initialDelaySeconds":60,"periodSeconds":10,"timeoutSeconds":5},"resources":{"limits":{"cpu":"1","memory":"2Gi"},"requests":{"cpu":"250m","memory":"1Gi"}},"volumeMounts":[{"mountPath":"/usr/share/elasticsearch/config/elasticsearch.yml","name":"es-config","subPath":"elasticsearch.yml"},{"mountPath":"/usr/share/elasticsearch/config/http-certs","name":"http-certs","readOnly":true},{"mountPath":"/usr/share/elasticsearch/data","name":"data"}]}],"initContainers":[{"command":["sh","-c","chown -R 1000:1000 /usr/share/elasticsearch/data"],"image":"busybox:1.35","name":"fix-permissions","volumeMounts":[{"mountPath":"/usr/share/elasticsearch/data","name":"data"}]}],"volumes":[{"configMap":{"items":[{"key":"elasticsearch.yml","path":"elasticsearch.yml"}],"name":"es-config"},"name":"es-config"},{"name":"http-certs","secret":{"secretName":"es-http-tls"}}]}},"volumeClaimTemplates":[{"metadata":{"name":"data"},"spec":{"accessModes":["ReadWriteOnce"],"resources":{"requests":{"storage":"5Gi"}}}}]}}
  creationTimestamp: "2026-06-23T22:41:26Z"
  generation: 5
  name: es-cluster
  namespace: elasticsearch
  resourceVersion: "965172"
  uid: a0e2a6b1-e372-4fed-96c0-f602a1d9a267
spec:
  persistentVolumeClaimRetentionPolicy:
    whenDeleted: Retain
    whenScaled: Retain
  podManagementPolicy: Parallel
  replicas: 3
  revisionHistoryLimit: 10
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: es-cluster
  serviceName: es-cluster
  template:
    metadata:
      annotations:
        kubectl.kubernetes.io/restartedAt: "2026-06-23T18:12:13-05:00"
      creationTimestamp: null
      labels:
        app: es-cluster
    spec:
      containers:
      - env:
        - name: POD_NAME
          valueFrom:
            fieldRef:
              apiVersion: v1
              fieldPath: metadata.name
        - name: ES_JAVA_OPTS
          value: -Xms512m -Xmx512m
        - name: ELASTIC_PASSWORD
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              key: password
              name: elastic-password
        image: docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:8.11.1
        imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
        name: elasticsearch
        ports:
        - containerPort: 9200
          name: http
          protocol: TCP
        - containerPort: 9300
          name: transport
          protocol: TCP
        readinessProbe:
          failureThreshold: 6
          initialDelaySeconds: 10
          periodSeconds: 5
          successThreshold: 1
          tcpSocket:
            port: 9200
          timeoutSeconds: 3
        resources:
          limits:
            cpu: "1"
            memory: 2Gi
          requests:
            cpu: 250m
            memory: 1Gi
        terminationMessagePath: /dev/termination-log
        terminationMessagePolicy: File
        volumeMounts:
        - mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/users
          name: file-realm
          subPath: users
        - mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/users_roles
          name: file-realm
          subPath: users_roles
        - mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/roles.yml
          name: file-realm
          subPath: roles.yml
        - mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/elasticsearch.yml
          name: es-config
          subPath: elasticsearch.yml
        - mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/http-certs
          name: http-certs
          readOnly: true
        - mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/data
          name: data
      dnsPolicy: ClusterFirst
      initContainers:
      - command:
        - sh
        - -c
        - chown -R 1000:1000 /usr/share/elasticsearch/data
        image: busybox:1.35
        imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
        name: fix-permissions
        resources: {}
        terminationMessagePath: /dev/termination-log
        terminationMessagePolicy: File
        volumeMounts:
        - mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/data
          name: data
      restartPolicy: Always
      schedulerName: default-scheduler
      securityContext: {}
      terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 30
      volumes:
      - name: file-realm
        secret:
          defaultMode: 420
          secretName: es-file-realm-aggregate
      - configMap:
          defaultMode: 420
          items:
          - key: elasticsearch.yml
            path: elasticsearch.yml
          name: es-config
        name: es-config
      - name: http-certs
        secret:
          defaultMode: 420
          secretName: es-http-tls
  updateStrategy:
    rollingUpdate:
      partition: 0
    type: RollingUpdate
  volumeClaimTemplates:

    metadata:

      name: data

content.thinking:
I can see:
1. The Elasticsearch StatefulSet mounts the `es-file-realm-aggregate` secret for file realm authentication (users, users_roles, roles.yml)
2. The `elastic` user password comes from the `ELASTIC_PASSWORD` env var (from `elastic-password` secret)
3. The ES uses HTTPS with self-signed certs

But wait, the `elastic` user's password is managed through the `ELASTIC_PASSWORD` env var at startup (bootstrap password) - but this is already a running cluster. After initial setup, the password is stored in Elasticsearch's internal security index.
Now I need to change the password from `elasticpass` to `elasticpass-elastic-password-next`. I'll call the Elasticsearch password API to update the elastic user, then patch the secret with the new password and restart the auth-checker pod to pick up the change. First, let me verify I can connect to ES.

content.text: Now I understand the setup. The `elastic` user password is in Elasticsearch's internal security index; I need to use the API to rotate it, then update the secret. Let me verify connectivity first.

content.thinking:
Good, the current password `elasticpass` works. Now let me:
1. Change the elastic user password to the new password using the ES API
2. Update the `elastic-password` secret to the new value
3. Restart the auth-checker to pick up the new secret

The new password is `elasticpass-elastic-password-next`.

content.text:
Current password works. Now I'll rotate the password in Elasticsearch, then update the secret, then bounce auth-checker.

**Step 1: Change the elastic user password via the ES API**

input.command:
NEW_PASS=$(kubectl get secret elastic-password-next -n elasticsearch -o jsonpath='{.data.password}' | base64 -d)
kubectl exec -n elasticsearch pod/curl-test -- curl -sk -u "elastic:elasticpass" \
  -X POST "https://es-http.elasticsearch.svc:9200/_security/user/elastic/_password" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d "{\"password\":\"${NEW_PASS}\"}" 2>&1
input.description: Rotate elastic user password in Elasticsearch

description: Rotate elastic user password in Elasticsearch

summary: Rotate elastic user password in Elasticsearch

content.text: Empty `{}` means success. **Step 2: Verify the new password works and old one fails**
New password returns 200, old returns 401. **Step 3: Update the `elastic-password` secret to the new value**

input.command:
NEW_PASS=$(kubectl get secret elastic-password-next -n elasticsearch -o jsonpath='{.data.password}' | base64 -d)
kubectl patch secret elastic-password -n elasticsearch \
  --type='json' \
  -p="[{\"op\":\"replace\",\"path\":\"/data/password\",\"value\":\"$(echo -n "$NEW_PASS" | base64)\"}]" 2>&1
input.description: Update elastic-password secret to new value

content.text: **Step 4: Restart auth-checker so it picks up the updated secret**

input.description: Wait for auth-checker rollout to complete

description: Wait for auth-checker rollout to complete

input.command:
for i in $(seq 1 24); do
  STATUS=$(kubectl get deployment auth-checker -n elasticsearch -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions[?(@.type=="Available")].status}' 2>/dev/null)
  READY=$(kubectl get deployment auth-checker -n elasticsearch -o jsonpath='{.status.readyReplicas}' 2>/dev/null)
  echo "Attempt $i: Available=$STATUS ReadyReplicas=${READY:-0}"
  if [ "$STATUS" = "True" ]; then
    echo "auth-checker is Ready!"
    break
  fi
  sleep 5
done
